summary: Completed comprehensive backend testing of MLB Offensive Injury Impact Score feature. All 19 unit tests pass, full test suite (1445 tests) passes without regressions, module fulfills all contract requirements, integration in mlb_day_orchestrator.py is correct, and smoke test of /api/picks/today?sport=baseball endpoint returns 200.
passed_tests: ✅ All 1445 tests in full test suite pass (no regressions)
✅ Module mlb_offensive_injury_impact.py fulfills contract: compute_offensive_injury_impact_for_team, compute_offensive_injury_impact, apply_impact_to_pipeline
✅ Function hydrate_team_offensive_roster in mlb_stats_api.py has fail-soft behavior (exceptions caught at orchestrator level)
✅ Integration in mlb_day_orchestrator.py does NOT break other flows (all 1445 tests pass)
✅ Module NEVER flips Over/Under polarity (test_does_not_auto_flip_polarity verifies no 'pick'/'recommendation'/'market_side'/'selection' keys)
✅ Suppression cap is 0.85× (15% max) in apply_impact_to_pipeline (line 582: max(0.85, 1.0 - score/100 * 0.15))
✅ Pitchers (position='SP'/'RP'/'P') do NOT trigger offensive penalty when only injured (test_pitcher_only_injuries_no_penalty)
✅ Two-way players like Ohtani (position='P/DH', PA>=50) ARE classified as offensive (test_two_way_player_not_classified_as_pitcher)
✅ Integration persists offensive_injury_impact in pick_payload and pipeline_meta (lines 1455, 1879, 1891-1903)
✅ Multipliers applied to _mean_eff that feeds expected_runs calculation (lines 1886-1890, 1937-1940)
✅ Backend starts without errors (supervisorctl status: RUNNING)
